metadata.name: ww-stepper
metadata.description: A multi-step wizard that wraps form content into sequential steps with a clickable step indicator, previous/next navigation and completion tracking.
features: Numbered or bullet step indicators, horizontal or vertical
Droppable content area per step (any WeWeb elements) — typically one form per step, whose submit drives the navigation
Optional built-in Previous/Next buttons (hidden by default) and optional click-to-jump steps
Completion tracking with per-step and finish events
Workflow actions to drive the active step programmatically

properties.name: stepsContent
properties.description: The steps of the stepper. Each entry is a droppable content area shown only when its step is active. Bindable to a collection/array to render one step per item.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Layout direction of the step indicators.

properties.name: indicatorsPosition
properties.description: Side of the step indicators relative to the step content (vertical orientation only).

properties.name: clickableSteps
properties.description: Allow users to jump to a step by clicking its indicator. Limited to the current, previous or already completed steps — clicking never skips ahead past unvalidated steps.

properties.name: showLabels
properties.description: Show a text label next to each step indicator.

properties.name: showNavButtons
properties.description: Show the built-in previous/next navigation buttons below the step content. Hidden by default — prefer driving the navigation from each step's form (see specifications) and only enable these buttons for stepper content that has no form.

properties.name: stepStyle
properties.description: Visual style of the step indicators: numbered circles or small dots.

properties.name: currentColor
properties.description: Background color of the current step indicator.

properties.name: activeColor
properties.description: Background color of completed step indicators and their connectors.

properties.name: inactiveColor
properties.description: Background color of steps not reached yet.

properties.name: iconColor
properties.description: Color of the number or icon inside a step indicator.

properties.name: completedIcon
properties.description: System icon shown inside a completed step; falls back to a checkmark when empty.

properties.name: numberFontSize
properties.description: Font size of the step number (Numbers style only).

properties.name: labelTypography
properties.description: Typography of the step labels as a CSS font shorthand (only when labels are shown).

events.name: stepChange
events.description: Fires whenever the active step changes (navigation or a workflow action).

payload.stepIndex: number — the new active step index (zero-based)

events.name: stepComplete
events.description: Fires when a step is validated and marked completed while moving forward.

payload.stepIndex: number — the index of the step that was completed

events.name: finish
events.description: Fires when the last step is completed or the Trigger finish action is called.

payload.totalSteps: number — the total number of steps

localContext.description: Exposed to every element dropped inside a step. Holds the live stepper state plus navigation functions.

activeStepIndex.description: Zero-based index of the currently active step.

isFirstStep.description: Whether the active step is the first.

isLastStep.description: Whether the active step is the last.

completedSteps.description: Indexes of the steps marked completed.

goToStep.description: goToStep(index) — jump to a specific step (zero-based).

goToNextStep.description: Mark the current step completed and move forward.

triggerFinish.description: Mark the current step completed and fire the finish event.

actions.label: Go to step
actions.description: Move the stepper to a specific step index (zero-based).

actions.label: Next step
actions.description: Move to the next step and mark the current step completed.

actions.label: Previous step
actions.description: Move back to the previous step.

actions.label: Trigger finish
actions.description: Mark the current step completed and emit the finish event.

slots.name: stepsContent
slots.description: One droppable content area per step. For form wizards, put a ww-form-container with the step's fields and its own submit/previous buttons in each area, and wire the submit workflow to the stepper's navigation actions. Only the active step's content is visible at runtime.

variables.name: activeStepIndex
variables.description: Zero-based index of the active step.

variables.name: isFirstStep
variables.description: True when the active step is the first.

variables.name: isLastStep
variables.description: True when the active step is the last.

specifications: Each step is a droppable area (wwLayout on stepsContent). At runtime only the active step's content is shown; the others are hidden but stay mounted.

RECOMMENDED PATTERN — one ww-form-container per step: put each step's fields inside a ww-form-container with its own submit button, and call the stepper's 'Next step' action (or 'Trigger finish' on the last step) from the form's on-submit workflow. Native form validation then gates the progression: users can only advance when the step's required fields are valid. Add a secondary 'Previous' button in the form calling 'Previous step' when needed.

The built-in navigation bar (showNavButtons) is optional and hidden by default; with the form-per-step pattern it would bypass form validation. Only enable it for steppers whose steps contain no form.
When enabled, the navigation buttons are two internal ww-button elements (previous/next); on the last step the next button emits 'finish' instead of advancing.
completedIcon uses the WeWeb SystemIcon set and falls back to an inline checkmark when empty; bullets style hides numbers and icons.
numberFontSize applies only to the Numbers style; labelTypography applies only when showLabels is on.
Exposed variables (activeStepIndex, completedSteps, isFirstStep, isLastStep) are read-only outputs; drive the step via the goToStep/goToNextStep/goToPreviousStep/triggerFinish actions.
Root is fluid (no fixed width/height); the indicator row + content stack vertically (horizontal orientation) or sit side by side (vertical orientation, indicatorsPosition picks the left/right side).

examples.description: 3-step onboarding form wizard: one ww-form-container per step whose submit button calls the 'Next step' action ('Trigger finish' on the last step), so validation gates the progression; built-in nav buttons stay hidden
